using wings and lifting surtaces operate atmosphere Spheroids are round egg-shaped and rely on their drive sys tems provide lift. Aerodynes are more maneuverable than spheroids, though the structural and control systems required limit their cargo capacity ad size_ drastically increasing their cost Of operation. Aerodynes also require 'ong runways which Iand; whereas spheroids can make VTOL landings under almost any conditions Spheroid DropShips are therefore more common; particularly for cargo transport and various mili- tary purposes_ The small size ad agility of aerodynes make tnem Idea attack craft, Microgravity uncomfortable ad unhealthy in the long term so most DropShips maintain facsimile of Terran gravity by constantly accelerating for half their journey between the JumpShip ad their destination_ At the haltway point, the cralt reverses its attitude placing its engines toward the intended Iandfall ad decelerating for the second half of the journey: This procedure allows the crew t0 operate the equivalent of Terran gravity for most of the voyage. also provides lastest means of system transit: albeit at the expense of fuel: Vessels short on reaction mass may accelerate sharply at the start of their journey; coasting much of the way t0 their des: tination: This inertial transter is much more fuel-efficient than the conventional method but it takes much longer, meaning that tne crew must spend extended periods without artificial gravity: This process has fallen out Of favor in recent decades, with the advent of more efficient fuel-flow systems
DropShips The term DropShip was coined in the twenty-second centu: ry t0 describe the huge_ cargo carrying shuttles transported by JumpShips  Over time_ these ships evolved into vessels carried on the null ot JumpShip and Iiterally dropped free upon arrival, Shipbuilders nave developed many DropShip designs over centuries: Dozens types remain service including attack   craft, cargo vessels and passenger liners However; every DropShip falls into one of two categories: aerodyne spheroid_ Aerodynes resemble conventionab aerospace craft,
